item.title: America faces an education depression. Why are Democrats silent?
item.description: The party has focused on defending institutions instead of empowering families.

item.title: The Iran war is eroding America’s China deterrent
item.description: U.S. tactical gains are consuming the ships, munitions and readiness needed for the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: The Iran war reveals the limits of U.S. military power
item.description: Many weaknesses can be fixed by smarter acquisitions decisions. Not so for better leadership.

item.title: I’ve experienced several types of warfare. Drones are the worst.
item.description: The safety that came with being on base between missions is a thing of the past.

item.title: Ukraine is at war. Its students won’t stop learning.
item.description: A university in Kyiv is under siege — and running 16 hours a day.

item.title: Burdensome regulations don’t make safer railroads
item.description: The White House’s new push for the Railway Safety Act flies in the face of its own talking points.

item.title: Hiking the minimum wage puts robots over people
item.description: The third time won’t be the charm in D.C.

item.title: When those who swore to protect choose to end a life
item.description: This week’s news in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Congestion pricing would cripple downtown D.C.
item.description: Mayoral candidates see drivers as enemies as they declare war on cars.

item.title: Katie Porter’s income tax fever dream
item.description: Narrowing the base won’t support the public services progressives want to provide.

item.title: What does it cost to die in America? Search your Zip code.
item.description: Calculate how much you should set aside for the final years of life.

item.title: An ‘epic fail’ by McDonald’s turned into a marketing win
item.description: The CEO’s video went viral for the wrong reasons. It still won the attention economy.

item.title: The Senate filibuster debate has lessons for Trump’s war on Iran
item.description: Deterrence can erode after one side escalates.

item.title: Taxing New York to death
item.description: America’s most famous mayor wants to give retirees another reason to flee his city.

item.title: Chicago careens toward insolvency
item.description: If politicians won’t fix bloated pensions amid credit downgrades, the bond market will force their hand.

item.title: The death of Bay Area public transportation
item.description: Massive service cuts loom despite a surge in local government spending.
